With this feature pack release, we continue to advance towards this goal via the following updates.

Feature enrichment for AI-assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning

In the previous two product releases, our teams have delivered a solid foundation for Predictive Labor Demand Planning (LDP), through which warehouse operators and shift managers can:

  • Predict planned workload on Outbound Delivery Order and Warehouse Order/Warehouse Task level based on historical data collected from RF Picking and Packing activities
  • Use a minimum of 2 weeks of historical data and predicts the short-term labor demand in next days or weeks
  • Utilize the SAP Smart Business dashboards to gain insights on workload duration, calculated Full-time-equivalents (FTE) and other important aspects such as volume, weight, and number of expected items

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01 release, the solution now supports the persistence of aggregated historical workload data and determines planned duration for outbound delivery staging.

Additionally, we have expanded the range of executed picking workload that can be captured – such as the start date and time - to improve LDP reporting, KPI calculation, and historical workload tracking for warehouse task confirmations via the external API. This has been done by integrating and enabling a two-step pick process for historical records and time tracking using an existing remote-enabled ODATA-API: API_WAREHOUSE_ORDER_TASK_2.

Also, on the topic of reporting for Labor Demand Planning, we now directly integrate the pLDP statistics to the Fiori App “Warehouse KPIs”, providing users with a view of workload forecast KPIs directly as configurable cards, alongside seamless navigation to the detailed smart-business drill-down apps.

The user experience is further enhanced through dynamic date functions. The first forecast graph now displays the total forecast value with drill-down to activity areas for deeper insight. Workload forecasts can be analyzed through five KPI measures—Duration, Volume, Weight, Capacity Consumption, and Number of Items—offering a clear and structured view of operational demand.

Joule integration with the EWM Outbound process

In the upcoming releases of Joule monthly schedule, we will release the full capabilities of Joule feature “Talk to your Warehouse Outbound process”, providing users with the possibility not only to retrieve data from EWM backend on Outbound Delivery Orders, but also to trigger next steps and accelerate the outbound fulfillment process. In addition, users can also navigate directly in the Joule conversation to the corresponding FIORI apps.

Get an overview on all available Joule capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition via our dedicated SAP Help – Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - highlighting transactional and informational capabilities to stay up-to-date.

ADVANCED SHIPPING & RECEIVING

Integration between transportation and warehouse logistics is a significant contributor towards logistics efficiency, synchronizing process changes between warehouse and transportation, reducing overall logistics-costs by optimizing and streamlining freight planning with warehouse execution. To further tighten this integration, with this release we provide the following enhancements and updates to Advanced Shipping & Receiving.

Perform ad-hoc loading

In this latest release, we further streamline the loading process by enabling warehouse operators to assign Outbound Delivery Orders to freight orders during the radio frequency (RF) loading process in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. When the first handling unit (HU) is scanned and loaded, the complete Outbound Delivery Order, including the remaining HUs, is assigned to the Freight Order. Ad-hoc loading can be performed in the following RF transactions:

  • Loading By HU
  • Loading By Door
  • Loading By Bill of Lading

This new process helps to simplify loading in a busy warehouse, in such scenarios as when:

  • There are regular freight orders to a certain plant or region. When the truck arrives, all the HUs in the staging area for this plant or region can be loaded onto it. This procedure is typically used in combination with the immediate loading process.
  • A truck at the door has some space left that can be further filled. Additional suitable HUs can be loaded onto the truck, and the system automatically assigns the corresponding Outbound Delivery Order to the Freight Order.

To see if an Outbound Delivery Order is relevant for Ad-Hoc Loading, users can check in the Outbound Delivery Orders app (transaction /SCWM/PRDO) and/or the Warehouse Monitor app (transaction /SCWM/MON).

Warehouse-Driven Outbound Delivery Order split

In the shipping process, sometimes companies need to split Handling Units based on actual pick, pack and stage status, according to various exceptions in the outbound process. To increase warehouse and shipping flexibility and allow the adjustment of deliveries according to operational reasons, we provide the option to do a warehouse-driven Outbound Delivery Order split with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition FPS01.

With this feature, warehouse and shipping operators can split Outbound Delivery Orders in the warehouse-driven scenario of Advanced Shipping and Receiving triggered from EWM. For example, for an urgent customer order and in case of stock insufficiency, it is now possible to split the Outbound Delivery Order into available quantity and the remaining quantity. So the available quantity can be sent to the customer as planned, and the remaining quantity can be sent at a late point in time, while the corresponding new split-Freight Unit can be considered for further planning in Transportation Management.

 

The split of Outbound Delivery Orders can take place up until the status Ready for Shipping is set. The Outbound Delivery Orders can be split in the following ways:

  • Split Outbound Delivery Order item into a new Outbound Delivery Order – the unpicked (and unpacked) item quantity is split from an Outbound Delivery Order into a new Outbound Delivery Order. For example, there is currently not enough quantity available in the warehouse, but the quantity that has already been picked could be shipped with an available transportation vehicle. The picked quantities stay in the original Outbound Delivery Order, and the unpicked quantities move to a new Outbound Delivery Order, including the corresponding Freight Unit in Transportation Management (TM).
  • Split handling unit (HU) into new Outbound Delivery Order – one or more selected HUs are split into a new Outbound Delivery Order. For example, an HU is to be delivered on a different date in the future. The HU, which is split, is moved to a new Outbound Delivery Order, while the remaining HUs in the original Outbound Delivery Order are kept in that Outbound Delivery Order. This scenario works in classic Advanced Shipping and Receiving loading as well as Immediate Loading.
  • Split remaining parts into new Outbound Delivery Order – the HUs in the Outbound Delivery Order that are to be delivered are selected, and the system splits the remaining items or HUs into a new Outbound Delivery Order. The selected HUs are kept in the original Outbound Delivery Order. For example, the HUs for an Outbound Delivery Order which are already fully picked, packed, and loaded to a specific transportation vehicle. However, the remaining parts of the Outbound Delivery Order will not be ready for picking, packing, or loading in time. The remaining parts are split to another Outbound Delivery Order and the loaded HUs are sent to the customer.

The setting for this functionality can be found in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Goods Issue Process > Outbound Delivery > Define Document Types for Outbound Delivery Process.  To enable splitting of Outbound Delivery Orders, Outbound Delivery Order Split must be chosen in the Outbound Split setting for the combination of document type and document category.

Harmonized SAP Global Trade Service integration

A final highlight I would like to mention in Advanced Shipping & Receiving is that the integration pattern for ASR and SAP Global Trade Services (SAP GTS) is now available for Extended Warehouse Management embedded in SAP S/4HANA. This integration pattern is harmonized with the integration pattern used in the decentralized setup for the Advanced Shipping and Receiving process (which is available since February 2025 with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release) and features enhance stability and reliability.

To enable the new integration pattern, users must ensure that the status type “DSC - LE Distribution Confirmation” is active in all relevant outbound delivery status profiles, in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Cross-Process Settings > Delivery - Warehouse Request > Status Management > Define Status Profiles.

SUITE INTEGRATION

Integration is a core pillar of our investments, by which we provide a deep and native integration with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud solution components, along with other SAP cloud solution offerings for seamless integration of comprehensive logistics end-to-end processes incl. production integration, customs processing and more. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 2025, FPS1 we deliver the following enhancements.

nJIT Supply-to-Customer (S2C)

Just-in-Time (JIT) processing is a common practice in manufacturing industries for efficient demand-driven production and logistics across supply chains. Just-In-Time Supply to Customer covers JIT processes from the perspective of a supplier. A JIT supply-to-customer(S2C) call is a request of the customer to deliver components to a ship-to location, specifying the requested quantity, date, time, and destination within the ship-to location for each item. The JIT S2C supply starts with the receipt of JIT calls from the customer. These JIT calls are directly relevant for shipping, assuming there is stock available from in-house production or external procurement. The shipping process starts with delivery creation and ends with posting goods issue. After goods issue, the settlement is done from creating the billing document until receiving the payment.

With the current release, we enable production planners, warehouse operators and shipping specialists to work with the S2C process for Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA in their storage locations. After the production and packing of the sequenced component groups, the system creates outbound deliveries using the Manage Customer JIT Calls by Component Groups app, including handling unit numbers from packing. These deliveries are then transferred to EWM. In EWM, the system creates an Outbound Delivery Order that contains the lowest-level HU details (to be used as pick-HUs) per delivery item. The HU warehouse tasks can be created manually or automatically to pick highest-level HUs and move them to the staging area. If HUs are already staged, the system automatically creates and confirms the corresponding pick tasks, reducing manual steps.

Quantity Correction Process in Advanced Returns Management (ARM)

There are cases when after material inspection is done for received customer returns, there rises a need to correct received quantities, for example when not the entire quantity was returned, or if upon inspection of the goods, some are beyond recovery and must be sent to salvage rather than storage.

With this release we enable logistical follow-up action “0023 - Goods Receipt Quantity Correction” to correct the goods receipt quantity after inspection so that discrepancies between the returned quantity and inspected quantity can be accurately reflected in the system. The outbound delivery is directly sent to Extended Warehouse Management with a corresponding document type and item type. Automatic warehouse task creation and confirmation to a staging zone, including an automatic goods issue posting is performed to enable the stock correction

To run the quantity correction process in Advanced Returns Management, users must activate business configuration set (BC set) Outbound Delivery Process for ARM Quantity Correction (/SCWM/DLV_OUTBOUND_ARM_QC). There is a proposed mapping for Logistics Execution (LE) document and item types to EWM document and item types in the BC set documentation.

Quality Inspections for Products in Outbound Delivery Orders

A final highlight worth spotlighting in the area of Suite Integration is the integration of quality management to outbound warehouse processes. With this integration, warehouse operators and quality inspectors can make sure that specific qualities of a product (for example, paperwork completion) are checked before the relevant Outbound Delivery Order leaves your warehouse. To this end, we introduced a new indicator on the EWM Outbound Delivery Order item – Relevant for Q-Inspection in Outbound.

With this indicator, the system checks the status of the quality inspection at goods issue posting in EWM and prevent successful posting in case quality inspection is not completed. The check prevents goods issue posting for EWM Outbound Delivery Orders with open inspection lots with origin:

  • 10 - Delivery to Customer with Sales Order
  • 11 - Delivery to Customer Without Sales Order
  • 12 - General Delivery

To use this feature, IOT 8 must be activated in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Cross-Process Settings > Quality Management > Basics and Integration > Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs.

CONTINUOUS MODERNIZATION & SIMPLIFICATION

Our commitment to modernization and continuous improvements not only enhances functionality but also provides users with a seamless and intuitive interaction, helping businesses drive efficiency, optimize processes, and deliver superior experiences to their stakeholders. To that end, I would like to highlight the following features from this latest release.

Use Stock Transfer Orders with Valuated Stock in Transit (SIT)

Starting with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 2025 FPS01 release, we enable the use of EWM-managed storage locations as receiving locations in the stock transfer process with valuated SIT. Valuated SIT can be used in both intra-company processes and processes that move stock between companies.

Quantify changes are not supported in the scenario, besides that an inbound delivery item with valuated SIT can be set to zero in a stock transfer order process, which cancels the delivery. Subsequently, the quantity of an inbound delivery item with valuated SIT cannot be changed to anything other than zero in a stock transfer order process.

The field EWMStockTransferOrderType is now also available in the OData API Warehouse Inbound Delivery - Read, Update (A2X) (API_WHSE_INB_DELIVERY_2). To enable this feature, the Prev. Qty Upd STO checkbox must be selected in Customizing for Extended Warehouse Management under Goods Receipt Process > Inbound Delivery > Define Item Types for Inbound Delivery Process.

Specify Handling Unit Number for Component Consumption

In certain production environments, the Production Supply Areas (PSA) are managed by the Handling Units. Before this release, production warehouse workers had to consume the entire component group HU at the PSA before you can register the consumption in the system. However, there are cases when only select sub-HUs are required from the PSA. In order to keep the physical world and the system in sync, we now allow users to specify which HU the component is to be consumed from. This strengthened integration is supported for synchronous goods movement postings via the Production Order Confirmation API (API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV), which passes the handling unit (HU) number for component consumption with synchronous goods movements.

CLEAN CORE & EXTENSIBILITY

The Clean Core Extensibility strategy aims to provide a modern and powerful integration option for customers and partners. Alongside supporting this strategy, we continuously adapt solution content to modernize and simplify the user experience. By regularly updating and refining Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, we ensure we remain relevant and effective in meeting current needs.

In the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release we continue to deliver on this with the following highlights:

  • Update for the Warehouse Order and Task (A2X) API – We have enhanced the existing ODATA-service Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), with the new ‘Lock/Unlock Warehouse Order’ actions on the Warehouse Order. This was done to enable efficient and lean integration of warehouse automation (e.g. robotics).
  • Enhancement of Stock List in the Warehouse Task Apps – We enabled warehouse operators to add their own fields to the stock list at warehouse task creation for warehouse request. For this, they can use the Business Add-In (BAdI) Enhancement for Stock List for Warehouse Task Creation (/SCWM/EX_CORE_CR_STOCK_LIST) to add additional fields to the display of stock at warehouse task creation for warehouse requests in the following apps:
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Inbound Delivery
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Outbound Delivery Order
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Posting Change
    • Create Warehouse Tasks - Stock Transfer

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above-mentioned topics as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release (such as RF improvement to allow RF receiving of mixed HUs with Serial Numbers, deletion of Inbound Deliveries in the STO scenario, and more) I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2025, Private Edition, FPS01” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2025 and SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: AI-assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning
entry.content:
As many of our EWM customers are excited to learn what AI capabilities we are working on, with S/4HANA Cloud for EWM, Private Edition, we’ve decided to write this blog to provide an overview on the AI-assisted predictive labor demand planning topic.

We hope it will give you good information on the released features, as well as hands-on information on how to start using it.

Feature scope of AI-assisted Predictive Labor Demand Planning (pLDP): 

  • 2023 FPS03 (release date Feb. 2025), we released the backend foundation for the pLDP in which users can get planned workload based on executed workload of RF based Picking and Packing activities in the warehouse outbound processes.
  • 2025 FPS00 (release date Oct. 2026), we introduced the Machine Learning algorithm in the EWM side to learn from the historical executed workload to derive future workload forecast. Meanwhile, we have built up a new UI on the Fiori based SAP Smart Business application to show the workload forecast with drill down capabilities.
  • 2025 FPS01 (release date Feb. 2026), we have just released the following feature enrichments in the pLDP:
    • RF-based Staging is also included in the forecast.
    • API for picking can be used for executed workload recording.
    • An introduction of aggregated historical workload data, to provide long-term forecasts for future releases.
    • The forecast cards are directly integrated into the FIORI Warehouse KPI app, with a refined UI

Where can I find more information on the released features? 
We have recorded the feature demos for AI-assisted predictive labor demand planning on our IRIS platform: 

  • https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_c6j2rizz  
  • https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_pgw47fje  
  • https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_hjlcr9hf  

Additionally, we recommend that you read our SAP Help documentation and Release Information Note for pLDP - SAP Note 3577311 

How can I configure the new Predictive Labor Demand Planning? 
Great question! Last year, we hosted a Meet the Expert session detailing how to activate the new feature. To view the recording here; please register with your company email. 
We've made a simple setup: just activate it in customizing and in the ISLM (Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management) FIORI app for your machine learning scenario. The EWM system automatically learns from then on, so no extra data training is required.

Do I need to use Labor Management (LM) to use the new Predictive Labor Demand Planning? 
No you do not need to use LM here. In the case that no Labor Management is active in the EWM managed warehouses, you can still use the new pLDP.  
Of course, using LM is compatible with the new pLDP. For example, if you maintain the ELS in Labor Management, the ELS will take over here as usually this activity is quite cost-intensive, so you do not want to lose that. But in the activity areas that ELS is not maintained, you can continue to use the new pLDP for the planned workload and workload forecast.  

How flexible is the workload forecast? 
Well, as usual, we provide our customers with different BADI enhancement possibilities, for you to influence the workload calculation as well as the interpretation of the forecast data. Our goal is that you can use these for workload planning according to the specific conditions that you have in the warehouses. You might want to check the SAP Help documentation for details. 

How accurate is the new Predictive Labor Demand Planning? 
We use a triple exponential smoothing algorithm in the Predictive Analysis Library to forecast the future workload based on historical workload, and so for data accuracy, data retention of the historical workload plays a key role here. In the future, we would like to provide also a comparison based on historical workload data with past forecasts. As of now, you can check it manually in the EWM system.  
Also, for longer-term forecasts other than the next weeks, it depends on the data availability of long term aggregated historical data. We will work on that and carefully choose the right Machine Learning algorithm for upcoming future releases.  

Which processes are supported with the new LDP feature? I would like to have the same process coverage in other warehouse processes. 
As of now, it is only supported for Outbound processes, for picking, packing and staging activities. You can also use POSC (Process-Oriented-Storage-Control) together with the new pLDP features. We are aware of the customer requirements to expand the workload forecast to other EWM processes, e.g. Inbound, Production Integration, etc. We will address this matter in future releases.  
Your feedback as valued customers and partners is greatly appreciated; please feel free to inform us of any ways we can further support your warehouse operations regarding this topic. 

How do I know if EWM managed warehouses are eligible for using the new AI-assisted predictive labor demand planning in our EWM managed warehouses?  
This is also mentioned in the central SAP Note for pLDP: SAP Note 3577311  
Make sure that you fulfil the requirement mentioned in SAP Note 3642983  

We are S/4HANA Cloud for EWM Private Edition customer, which releases do I need to be able to use the new LDP feature? 
The minimal requirement for the eligible release is S/4HANA Cloud for EWM, Private Edition 2022 FPS0, and later releases. We have downported the 2023 FPS03 release scope already. For 2025 FPS0 release scope, we plan a downport which comes very soon. For the 2025 FPS01 release scope, we will check based on customer requirements to decide for the downport in the future. So, feel free to leave a comment.  

I have tried the new LDP feature and would like to provide feedback and future development requirements. How can I reach you? 
Thanks! This is exactly what we are looking for!  
You are always welcome to leave a comment in this blog post or write to us in private messages. Please leave your contact information and we will contact you shortly to discuss the details.  

Further reads on what's new in 2025 FPS01:

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-extended-warehouse-management-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition/ba-p/14336378

entry.title: Advanced intercompany stock transfer with valuated stock-in-transit(VSiT) for difference in receipt
entry.content:
Valuated stock-in-transit (VSiT) means that the goods have left the plant of the seller physically, but the ownership (title or risk) of goods has not yet been transferred from the seller to the buyer i.e the seller still holds ownership of goods. Ownership is transferred at some point during the transit generally at the incoterm location or at a location mutually agreed between the seller and buyer.

Advanced intercompany stock transfer uses VSiT to transfer the ownership of goods between delivering company , intermediate company (in case of multistage ) and receiving company.

There was a limitation in confirmation of goods receipt with difference in quantity for EWM managed storage location till S/4HANA2025 release as stated in SAP note 1822497 as in below image. This limitation has been eliminated in 2025 FPS01 release. My present blog is to discuss the new feature in context of advanced intercompany stock transfer(This new feature also works with classic intercompany stock transfer and intracompany stock transfer).

A typical process flow for VSiT with advanced intercompany stock transfer with delivering company 1810 US and receiving company 1010 DE is depicted in below image.

An Intercompany purchase order 4500003515 with quantity 5pc of purchase order type NBIC (IC SiT STO) is created at receiving plant 1010 with EWM managed storage location EROD at purchasing organization 1010 DE receiving company 1010 DE with intercompany vendor BP1810 representing delivering company 1810 US.

An outbound delivery 80004929 of delivery type NCC3 is created at US delivering plant 1810 with storage location UAFS.

Goods issue is executed on the corresponding outbound delivery order (ODO) 80004931 at EWM warehouse U002 at delivering company US.

Goods issue on EWM ODO 80004931 triggered PGI (Post gods issue) on LE delivery 80004931 which subsequently triggered VSiT postings (with movement types 681, 685, 107) and created inbound delivery 180001751 as shown with app Monitor value chains.

 

The inbound delivery 180001751 is created automatically with delivery type ELST (IDel. Val. Rec. SiT) at receiving plant 1010 DE with storage location EROD .

The new feature of 2025 FPS01 allows receipt of different quantity with exception code like DIFW (Diff. as Charges for Warehouse) but not with DIFD (Diff. as Charges for Inbound Delivery) as DIFD updates delivery quantity. A warehouse task is created on the inbound delivery  180001751 and the error Quantity change is not allowed with valuated SIT in an STO process.Message No. /SCWM/DELIVERY758 appears when confirming 4 pc for delivery quantity 5 pc with exception code DIFD.

System accepts the confirmation of 4 pc with exception code DIFW.

Difference in quantity needs to be updated with difference analyzer /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER.

The pre-requisite configuration to achieve the functionality is to flag the new checkbox “Prevent quantity changes for stock transport order” for the inbound item type IDLV for document category PDI.

There isa new field at the item level of the inbound delivery indicating the STO type (E Advanced intercompany process with valuated stock in transit).

Purchase order history shows the relevant movement types for VSiT.

This blog is based on my personal tests , observations , insights in S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release. Will appreciate your feedback / comments.

entry.title: SAP EWM Transaction /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG
entry.content:
SAP EWM Transaction Report: /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG

Summary

  • The /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG transaction enables automated modification of stock attributes in SAP Extended Warehouse Management without physical stock movement
  • Key capabilities include stock type changes, inspection document creation, and consignment stock conversion with comprehensive background processing support
  • Primary use cases encompass quality management processes, expired inventory handling, and automated consignment stock management

Transaction Overview

Transaction Code

  • Primary: /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG (Change Stock Attributes)
  • Alternative: Program /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE via SE38
  • Menu Access: SAP Easy Access → Logistics → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Execution → Change Stock Attributes

 

Business Purpose

  • Core business objective: Automate stock attribute modifications on one-off or recurring basis to support warehouse operations and compliance requirements
  • Functional area coverage: Extended Warehouse Management with integration into Quality Management and ERP systems
  • Integration points: SAP ERP for goods movements, Quality Management for inspection processes, and Financial Accounting for inventory valuation

System Requirements

  • SAP Version: S/4HANA only (available since SAP S/4HANA 2020 FPS01)
  • Required Modules: SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), optional Quality Management (QM) integration
  • Prerequisites: EWM warehouse configuration, appropriate authorization objects, and tolerance group assignments

Functional Capabilities

Core Features

  1. Stock Type Modification: Change stock types (e.g., F2 to Q4, F2 to B6) without physical movement
  2. Inspection Document Creation: Generate quality inspection documents with IOT 5 (Q-Inspection Product/Batch Whse-Internal)
  3. Consignment Stock Conversion: Automatically post consignment stock to own stock after specified time periods

Processing Options

  • Stock Type Change Mode: Modify stock attributes while maintaining physical location
  • Inspection Creation Mode: Create inspection documents and post to QM stock types
  • Consignment Posting Mode: Convert vendor consignment stock to company-owned inventory

Selection Parameters

Parameter

Description

Required

Notes

Warehouse Number

EWM warehouse identifier

Yes

Defines processing scope

Stock Type From

Source stock type

Conditional

Required for stock type changes

Stock Type To

Destination stock type

Conditional

Required for stock type changes

Material

Material number or range

No

Enables selective processing

Storage Location

ERP storage location

No

Additional filtering criteria

Movement Reason

Business reason code

Conditional

Mandatory for certain modes

Date Range

Selection by goods receipt/inspection dates

No

Time-based filtering

Navigation and Access

Menu Path

  • SAP Easy Access → Logistics → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Execution → Change Stock Attributes

Alternative Access Methods

  • Direct Transaction: /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG
  • Program Execution: SE38 → /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE
  • Background Processing: Schedule as background job for automated execution

Configuration Requirements

Authorization Objects

  • /SCWM/TOCR: Warehouse task creation authorization with Warehouse Process Type restrictions
  • Tolerance Group Assignment: Background users must be assigned via Extended Warehouse Management → Settings → Physical Inventory → Assign User to Tolerance Group for Difference Analyzer

Master Data Prerequisites

  • Inspection Rules: IOT 5 (Q-Inspection Product/Batch Whse-Internal) configuration for quality management scenarios
  • Storage Type Configuration: Mixed owner settings for consignment stock processing
  • Material Master: Complete material master data with appropriate stock management settings

System Configuration

  • Warehouse Process Type: Define whether warehouse tasks are created automatically during posting changes
  • Default Values: Configure destination data for stocks in conjunction with warehouse process types

Step-by-Step Usage Guide

Preparation Steps

  1. Verify tolerance group assignment for background processing users
  2. Ensure inspection rules are configured for quality management scenarios
  3. Validate storage type settings for mixed owner scenarios

Execution Process

  1. Access Transaction: Navigate to /SCWM/STOCK_ATT_CHG or execute program /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE
  2. Configure Selection Parameters: Enter warehouse number, stock types, materials, and date ranges
  3. Choose Processing Mode: Select stock type change, inspection creation, or consignment posting
  4. Executed Report: Run in foreground for testing or schedule as background job for automation

Post-Processing Activities

  • Review Logs: Analyze detailed processing results and error messages
  • Monitor Warehouse Tasks: Track generated warehouse tasks for physical movements
  • Verify Integration: Confirm ERP posting and quality management document creation

Output and Results

Report on Output Format

  • Detailed logs showing successful stock type changes, created inspection documents, and warehouse tasks
  • Comprehensive tracking of processed items with original and new stock attributes
  • Error reporting for failed processing with specific failure reasons

Generated Documents

  • Warehouse Tasks: For physical movements when required
  • Inspection Documents: Quality management inspection lots
  • ERP Material Documents: Goods movement postings for inventory updates

System Updates

  • Stock Attribute Changes: Updated stock types, inspection status, and ownership
  • ERP Integration: Synchronized inventory values and stock classifications

Business Scenarios

Use Case 1: Consignment Stock Conversion

  • Objective: Automatically convert vendor consignment stock to own stock after 90 days
  • Process: Schedule background job to identify consignment stock older than 90 days and convert to own stock
  • Outcome: Reduced manual intervention and improved cash flow management

Use Case 2: Quality Management Integration

  • Objective: Create inspection documents for batch-managed materials requiring quality control
  • Process: Post unrestricted stock to quality inspection stock and generate inspection lots
  • Outcome: Systematic quality control with full traceability and compliance

Use Case 3: Expired Inventory Management

  • Objective: Block stock with expired shelf-life expiration dates
  • Process: Automatically convert unrestricted stock to blocked stock based on expiration criteria
  • Outcome: Prevention of non-compliant product distribution and regulatory compliance

Integration Points

ERP Integration

  • Goods Movement Posting: Automatic creation of material documents in ERP for inventory updates
  • Financial Integration: Synchronization with Financial Accounting for inventory valuation

Related SAP Modules

  • Quality Management (QM): Integration for inspection lot creation and quality stock management
  • Materials Management (MM): Stock type changes reflected in inventory management

External System Interfaces

  • Warehouse Management Systems: Integration with external WMS for physical execution
  • Quality Systems: Connection to external quality management platforms

Troubleshooting Guide

Common Issues

Issue

Symptoms

Root Cause

Resolution

Complete Processing Failure

All items fail to process

Single line with errors (e.g., obsolete material status)

Validate all materials before execution to prevent all-or-nothing failure

Performance Issues

Extended processing times

Large dataset processing

Break into smaller batches or schedule during off-peak hours

XML Processing Errors

Special character handling failures

Bin names contain special characters like "/$"

Implement BAdIs QIE_ELEMENT_PROP_CONVERT/QIE_INSP_DOC_PROP_CONVERT

Error Messages

  • Error Code: Material Status Obsolete
    • Description: Cannot process materials with obsolete status
    • Resolution: Update material master data or exclude obsolete materials from selection

Performance Considerations

  • Dataset Size: Large datasets can cause extended processing times requiring batch processing
  • Background Processing: Use background jobs for recurring operations to avoid system performance impact
  • Tolerance Group Configuration: Proper tolerance group assignment prevents authorization issues

Best Practices

Operational Guidelines

  • Data Validation: Always validate master data completeness before execution to prevent processing failures
  • Batch Processing: Use smaller batches for large datasets to improve performance and error handling
  • Background Scheduling: Schedule recurring processes during off-peak hours to minimize system impact

Security Considerations

  • Authorization Management: Implement proper authorization controls using /SCWM/TOCR and warehouse process type restrictions
  • Tolerance Group Assignment: Ensure appropriate tolerance group assignments for different handling

Data Management

  • Master Data Integrity: Maintain complete and accurate master data to prevent processing errors
  • Configuration Consistency: Ensure consistent configuration across warehouse process types and storage types

Related Transactions

Transaction

Description

Relationship

/SCWM/POST

Posting Change for Warehouse Number

Direct posting change functionality

/SCWM/MON

Warehouse Monitor

Stock monitoring and manual attribute changes

/SCWM/ERP_STOCKCHECK

ERP Stock Check

Inventory synchronization between EWM and ERP

Technical Specifications

Program Details

  • Program Name: /SCWM/R_STOCK_TYPE_CHANGE
  • Package: SAP Extended Warehouse Management
  • Object Type: Report program with background processing capabilities

Database Tables

  • Stock Tables: EWM stock management tables for attribute updates
  • Warehouse Task Tables: Generated warehouse task records
  • Integration Tables: ERP interface tables for goods movement posting

Enhancement Points

  • BAdI /SCWM/EX_CORE_GM_STOCK: Custom authority checks for stock posting
  • Quality Management BAdIs: QIE_ELEMENT_PROP_CONVERT and QIE_INSP_DOC_PROP_CONVERT for special character handling

Conclusion and Recommendations

Key Benefits

  • Process Automation: Eliminates manual stock attribute changes, reducing errors and processing time
  • Compliance Support: Ensures regulatory compliance through automated quality management and expiration handling
  • Integration Excellence: Seamless integration with ERP and Quality Management systems

Hope that can help you.

Thank you and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Quality inspection at outbound with Extended warehouse management (EWM)
entry.content:
Inspection at outbound is demanded by many customers for specific products. Inspection on EWM managed outbound delivery was not possible till S/4HANA 2025 FPS0 release. Capability of inspection on outbound delivery with EWM has been introduced with S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release. My present blog is to discuss on the functionality and the prerequisite configurations to achieve inspection with EWM managed outbound delivery.

A typical process flow for inspection for EWM managed outbound delivery is depicted in below image.

An outbound delivery order (ODO) 80004946 relevant for Q-Inspection at outbound at item level has been picked , packed but not yet goods issued.

An inspection lot 100000000050 has been created in S/4HANA for the material FG1015 for the outbound delivery 80004946 from sales order 10005725.

If the goods issue is triggered on the EWM ODO, then an error message appears aborting the goods issue as the usage decision on inspection lot is pending or the inspection lot has not been accepted.

Pre-requisite configuration and master data are

  • Inspection object type 8 (Q-Inspection Outbound) needs to be activated at SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Basics and Integration -> Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs.

  • Inspection lot origin needs to be assigned to delivery type

  • Inspection type needs to be assigned to delivery category

  • Quality info record needs to be created for the material with customer and sales org.

This functionality also works for

  • Embedded and decentral EWM (with backend S/4HANA 2025 FPS01)
  • Delivery type LO Delivery w/o Ref.
  • Material with batch management

QM with EWM outbound is a simple and useful solution closing a gap for feature parity of EWM with IM.

This blog is based on my personal tests , observations , insights in S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release. Will appreciate your feedback / comments.

entry.title: Modernization and simplification of SAP EWM on SAP S4 HANA 2025 FPS01 Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
With S4 HANA EWM 2025 FSP01,  below Functional enhancements are part of SAP Extended Warehouse Management for use with SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 Version

  • Support consumption posting (movement type 241) against assets
  • Specify the handling unit number during synchronous production and process order consumption
  • Receive mixed handling units through radio frequency transactions
  • Extend the stock list during warehouse task creation
  • Support alternative units of measure for the warehouse stock migration object in SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit 

Support consumption posting (movement type 241) against assets: Prior to S4 HANA 2025 FPS01, asset-related fields were not available  in delivery structures (LIPS) or passed through the delivery creation interface (KOMDLGN). 

Earlier the delivery items which are stored in the table LIPS, which does not contain an asset field, however the asset information is mandatory when posting 241 movement. As a result the asset number is not read by function module MB_CREATE_GOODS_MOVEMENT.

This was mentioned in SAP Note-2603290 - M8147 during EWM / ECC posting with movement 241 - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA.

From S4 HANA 2025 FPS01, the delivery communication structure KOMDLGN is extended with the asset fields ANLN1 (main asset number) and ANLN2 (asset subnumber). These fields are populated from the goods movement data (MSEG) during delivery generation and persisted in the delivery item data (LIPS). During post goods issue, the same fields are mapped back from the delivery to the goods movement interface to ensure that the asset information is available for final posting.

LIPS table is extended to Store Asset Information.

A new delivery DOG3 is mapped to post asset posting against delivery.

Even in Synchronous Movement ERP2EWM class /SCWM/CL_EWM_GOODSMVT_SYNC & BAPI BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE, MIGO is enriched to pass asset information to EWM and conduct direct 241 Movement with material document.

Specify the handling unit number during synchronous production and process order consumption: 

Scope
• Enable for Handling Unit managed Production Staging Area (PSA) to define an exact Handling Unit (e.g. a sub-Handling Unit) for production consumption.
• Supported for synchronous goods movement postings in Production Order Confirmation API.
• Extension of existing BAPIs with Handling Unit parameter:
• BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR –Process Order
• BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_HDR – Production Order
• API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SR

The details and specifics of HU based consumption based posting is mentioned in SAP note-3702263 - Manufacturing Component Consumption from HU with synchronous goods movements using BAPIS and ODATA APIs

Below test case is replicated for Process Order Synchronous HU based Consumption. BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR is modified to pass HU information from S4 against process order/PMR.

The process order is consumed with HU- 803951 passed in BAPI-BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR

However, the Material document in S4 is not updated with HU.

As per SAP NOTE-3672337 - Enhance the Inventory Management objects with EWM Handling Unit information, only BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE & below Idocs are enhanced to update HU information in S4 material document.

EWM_HUIDENT, Type: /SCWM/DE_HUIDENT BAPI function modules that have been enhanced with warehouse handling unit information:

  • MAP2I_B2017_GM_ITEM_TO_IMSEG( BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE)
  • MP2E_IMSEG_TO_2017_ITEM_CREATE( BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE)
  • IDoc Segment that has been enhanced: E1BP2017_GM_ITEM_CREATE1

Lets Try HU based consumption in process order using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE

Bapi is executed and HU based posting is stored in Material document as well

Hence BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE can only update HU specific Posting in S4 MATDOC if customer intends to have this value stored. However, BAPI BAPI_PROCORDCONF_CREATE_HDR can still be used if customer expectation is just to specific HU in Mnaufacturing order and update Confirmation parameters( PCNF, CNF). However, HU information will not be stored in MSEG table PP Confirmations Bapis.

Improved RF Receiving of Mixed HUs with Serial Numbers

Scope
• New button to maintain Serial Numbers for additional products in RF Receiving into a mixed HU that already contains Serial Numbers-managed products.
• Support serial number requirement on bin stock “C Serial Num. Req. in IM”
• Update of Inbound Delivery document with newly packed products/SNs.

Key Benefits
• Enhance the flow of inbound processing for mixed handling units
• Efficiently pack multiple products with serial number requirement into a Handling Unit (HU).

The other 2 below topics would be covered in next part of blog.

  • Extend the stock list during warehouse task creation
  • Support alternative units of measure for the warehouse stock migration object in SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit 

All tests are performed in S4 HANA 2025 FPS01 Sandbox system.

entry.title: Use case: Event-Driven Integration of Robotic Tire Palletizing with Cimcorp, MES, and SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Author: Naveen Chandra Sharma

(Naveen is a SAP Solution architect having 29+ years of experience which includes 20+ years into SAP consulting with expertise in SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA and integration with other external system like MES, CIMPRO etc. He worked on various type of project flavors like Implementation, Enhancement and support projects in various industries Manufacturing, Finance, Pharmaceutical, Food, Telecommunication)

 1.Abstract:

This use case considers a tire manufacturing environment in which finished tires moves down through a conveyor channel to a warehouse staging area. Within the staging area, multiple robotic units are installed to automatically pick the individual tires from the conveyor and load them onto pallets. The robotic palletizing process continues until the pallet reaches its predefined capacity limit. Once the pallet is fully loaded, it is transferred to the end of the staging area where it becomes available for warehouse handling operations. A warehouse operator subsequently retrieves the completed pallet and executes the put away process within the warehouse management system, ensuring proper storage and inventory registration.

The overall system landscape consists of four primary systems: SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA with decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the Manufacturing Execution System (MES), and the Cimcorp warehouse automation system. SAP PI/PO serves as the middleware layer to enable communication and data exchange between MES and the decentralized S/4HANA EWM system. This document presents a structured architecture describing the integration and interaction among the aforementioned systems.

Above architecture works well with event driven integration model, which ensures the transactional consistency between MES, CIMPRO and EWM system during whole tire palletization process.

2.0 Introduction:

Example: The tire production line operates at a very high speed and runs continuously 24×7 at full capacity. On average, one tire moves to the staging area every two seconds, resulting in approximately 30 tires per minute. Since the staging area has limited space, automation becomes essential to handle the high production rate efficiently. Without automated handling and palletizing systems, tires would quickly accumulate in the staging area, leading to operational congestion and potential disruption of the production flow.

Another approach would be going without structured integration, robotic systems often operate as isolated system, which restricts real-time inventory visibility and reconciliation challenges between production systems and Warehouse system.

This case study proposes a standardized integration framework connecting a Cimcorp robotic palletizing system with a Manufacturing Execution System (MES) and SAP S/4HANA integrated with embedded SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). This architecture enables the following:

• Real-time pallets build confirmation
• Pallet handling unit creation
• Serial-level traceability for tiers
• Goods receipt automation
• Warehouse bin put-away
• End-to-end production-to-warehouse synchronization

The proposed design supports scalable automation environments commonly found in modern tire manufacturing plants.

3.0 System Architecture:

a. Enterprise Resource Planning Layer (ERP Layer)

This layer manages enterprise-wide business processes and master data.

Systems:

  • SAP ECC – Legacy ERP system responsible for financials, material master data, sales orders, and enterprise reporting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Material master and business master data management
  • Financial and sales transactions
  • Inventory visibility at enterprise level
  • Integration with warehouse and production systems

b. Warehouse Management Layer

This layer handles warehouse execution and logistics operations.

System:

  • SAP S/4HANA with Decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM)

Key Responsibilities

  • Warehouse task creation and management
  • Inventory tracking and stock movements
  • Put away and storage bin management
  • Handling unit and pallet management
  • Integration with warehouse automation systems

c. Manufacturing Execution Layer

This layer manages shop-floor production control and operational execution.

System:

  • Manufacturing Execution System (MES)

Key Responsibilities

  • Production order execution
  • Batch and lot tracking
  • Shop-floor monitoring and control
  • Production reporting and traceability
  • Communication with warehouse and automation systems

d. Warehouse Control and Automation Layer

This layer controls physical automation equipment on the warehouse floor.

System:

  • Cimcorp Warehouse Control System (WCS)

Key Responsibilities

  • Control of robotic palletizing systems
  • Conveyor and buffer management
  • Pallet creation and movement
  • Real-time equipment monitoring
  • Communication with MES for production and pallet data
  • (Note: although PI/PO is not the best option for S4HANA but as PI/PO was already using in ECC application, so used this one here)

4.0 Interesting facts about Tire:

Size of tire, which is imposed on tire physically, which is very much visible to all. This help to understand the exact width, Aspect ratio and Rim diameter for each tire.

 

 

And below summary, shows tire types, main features and major manufactures.

5.0 Event-Driven Integration Model:

5.1 Pallet build lifecycle:

5.1.1 Assumptions:

  • Each robot has a unique robot number and dedicated staging area
    • Each pallet has a unique pallet HU identifier
    • Each tire has a unique serial number encoded as a HU
    • Tiers with identical characteristics share the same material number
    • Each pallet has a predefined capacity stored within MES and WCS logic

5.1.2 Palletization steps:

 

Step 1: Tier Arrival Detection

A sensor or vision system detects tire arrival. The robot scans the pallet identifier and the tire serial number before pallet placement.

Step 2: Robotic Pallet Placement

The robot places tiers on the pallet following a predefined stacking pattern.

Step 3: WCS Event Recording

The WCS records the pallet build information including:

  • Pallet ID
    • Serial number(tyre serial)
    • Material number
    • Date and time
    • Quantity
  • Stock Type
  • Date of manufacturing
  • Date of expiry

 

Step 4: Pallet Completion

Once the pallet reaches the defined capacity, the robot moves the pallet to an outbound conveyor staging area. And then WCS send MES, pallet information and following this, MES updates this record and complete order. marks the pallet as complete in their record.

 

Step 5: XML Message Transmission

  • The pallet information is transmitted to MES and SAP S/4HANA using an XML interface. In this scenario, we are using the Push mechanism, wherein MES is sending Pallet file in XML format though middle layer(SAP PI/PO) then respective pallet information is saved in a file under Input folder.
  • From MES side: Once XML file goes to SAP/PI/PO then that file, moves from process folder to achieve folder
  • As soon as XML file saves in file under SAP, then parallel information goes to queue(can verify in SMQ2/SMQ1) and process the each pallet.

 

Example:

Step 6: Tire Handling Unit Creation

In SAP, each tire serial number is created as an individual handling unit with expiration characteristics (Added below fields in /SCWM/HUITM)

Step 7: Pallet Handling Unit Creation

A pallet handling unit is generated, and the tire handling units are packed under the pallet. This step will develop a parent child relation between Tire(HU) and Pallet(HU). Once one pallet is processed then that file will move from Inbound to archive folder.

Assumptions:

  • Each tire serial number is unique and HU is presenting the same in SAP
  • HU is also presenting Pallet in SAP.
  • Number range of both pallets are different to make this very clear to everyone.

Step 8: Warehouse Put-Away

The pallet handling unit is automatically put away into the warehouse storage bin using SAP EWM warehouse task processing.

Below is example, how stock looks like(with pallet HU & Child HU):

 

 

6.0 Error handling in S4HANA:

1)At any point of time, if someone want to check, that whether/how many XML messages received in S4HANA, which can be verified by using tcode : SXMB_MONI

As plant is working on 24 x 7then messages should come very frequently like 1-2 minutes, if it is not then need to check interface.

 

 

 

2)In case tire serial(Tire HU) number already exist then this pallet(HU) would be in error queue. Someone need to check the queue in frequent interval to avoid the errors.

3)Sometime, if XML is missing some information like stock type or quantity or some field is blank, then also pallet would be in queue error.

4) Sometime, if XML is populating wrong material number, missing some information like stock type or quantity or some field is blank, then also pallet would be in queue error.

7.0 Data Objects and Traceability:

The integration synchronizes both master and transactional data objects. This structure ensures complete traceability from production line to warehouse storage.

Master Data

  • Material master (material number of Tire type): There is mapping table for MES material number and SAP material/Product number.
    • Storage location(need to correctly align with storage type/Availability group)
    • Warehouse bins

Transactional Data

  • Handling unit (Tire serial number)
    • Handling unit (Pallet)
    • Warehouse stock

1.There is a mapping between MES stock type and SAP EWM stock type:

MES

EWM

Free      

P2(Unrestricted)

Blocked

B2(Blocked)

8.0 Reliability and Transactional Integrity:

To ensure robust system operation, the integration framework implements the following safeguards.

  • Idempotent message processing to prevent duplicate postings
    • Store-and-forward queuing in MES and Cimcorp to tolerate ERP downtime
    • Error quarantine handling for label scanning failures or quantity mismatches
    • Periodic reconciliation between MES pallet records and SAP inventory postings

These mechanisms ensure consistent synchronization between shop-floor events and enterprise inventory records

9.0 Conclusion:

This Use case, integration of a robotic tire palletizing cell with Cimcorp automation, MES, and SAP S/4HANA establishes a digitally synchronized production-to-warehouse workflow. By implementing this event-driven architecture and structured handling unit management, the system provides:

  • Real-time inventory visibility – As soon Tire is manufactured in production, with the help of palletization, we can easily check the inventory.
    • Serial-level (HU based) traceability each product - At Tire HU level Manufacturing date, expiry date, country of origin. If product is manufactured in multiple locations - At tire level, one can easily identify, where tire was manufactured. One can easily identify the expiry date of each tire, which will help to restrict to dispatch the expired tire to customer.
    • Automated warehouse execution specially in palletization - Automated warehouse execution, particularly in palletization, plays a key role in modern manufacturing and distribution environments. In such systems, finished goods coming from production lines are automatically arranged and stacked onto pallets using robotic palletizers. Sensors and conveyor systems transport the products to palletizing stations where robots organize them according to predefined stacking patterns to ensure stability and optimal space utilization.
    • Scalable automation integration - Scalable automation integration enables warehouses and manufacturing facilities to expand their automation capabilities as operational demands grow. By designing systems with modular and flexible architectures, organizations can easily add new equipment such as conveyors, robotic palletizers, without disrupting existing operations. Integration with enterprise platforms like SAP Extended Warehouse Management ensures that automated equipment can communicate seamlessly with warehouse processes, inventory management, and order fulfilment activities. This approach supports real-time data exchange, efficient material flow control, and centralized monitoring of automated systems.
